description: Flows Discovery & Export Enhancements — add Name/Visibility to export dialog, /flows discovery page with mini canvas previews, and Discover section on home page

userStories.title: Extract demo→flow conversion to shared util
userStories.description: As a developer, I need convertNode and convertConnector extracted from view-canvas.tsx into apps/viewer/src/lib/demo-to-flow.ts so both view-canvas and the upcoming MiniCanvas can share them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/viewer/src/lib/demo-to-flow.ts exporting convertNode and convertConnector (with STYLE_BY_KIND, STYLE_BY_STYLE constants)
Remove the private convertNode, convertConnector, STYLE_BY_KIND, STYLE_BY_STYLE from apps/viewer/src/components/view-canvas.tsx
view-canvas.tsx imports convertNode and convertConnector from ../lib/demo-to-flow
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add FlowListItem types and viewer API helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need FlowListItem and FlowsResponse types in types.ts, a viewer-api.ts module with fetchFlow and fetchFlows helpers, and flow-view.tsx refactored to use fetchFlow.
acceptanceCriteria: Append FlowListItem (uuid, name, createdAt, demo: Demo) and FlowsResponse (flows, total, page, totalPages) to apps/viewer/src/types.ts
Create apps/viewer/src/lib/viewer-api.ts with fetchFlow(uuid) and fetchFlows(page, limit) functions pointing to https://seeflow.dev/api
apps/viewer/src/pages/flow-view.tsx uses fetchFlow from viewer-api.ts and removes its inline API_BASE constant and raw fetch call
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add name and visibility params to exportToCloud
userStories.description: As a developer, I need exportToCloud to accept name and visibility params, use the zip key seeflow.json (public) or seeflow.private.json (link), and include name in the API query string.
acceptanceCriteria: exportToCloud signature is (projectId, email, name, visibility: 'public' | 'link')
Visibility 'public' puts demo JSON in seeflow.json; 'link' puts it in seeflow.private.json
name is sent as URL query param alongside email
Visibility type is exported as Visibility from use-export-to-cloud.ts
All existing tests updated to pass name and visibility args
New test: visibility 'link' produces seeflow.private.json in zip (not seeflow.json)
New test: name appears in cloud API URL query params
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Name, Visibility fields and View button to export dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to name my flow, choose its visibility, and open the shared link directly from the export dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: Export dialog has a Name text input (required, localStorage key seeflow.export.name) placed above Visibility
Export dialog has a Visibility select with options 'Public — anyone can discover it' (value public) and 'Anyone with the link' (value link), persisted in localStorage key seeflow.export.visibility
Export button is disabled when name is empty (in addition to existing email check)
After successful export, a View button (ExternalLink icon) opens shareUrl in a new tab alongside the existing copy button
useExportToCloud hook is called with email, name, visibility
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add MiniCanvas non-interactive preview component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a MiniCanvas component in apps/viewer that renders a Demo as a locked-down, non-interactive React Flow canvas for use inside flow cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/viewer/src/components/mini-canvas.tsx
MiniCanvas accepts a demo: Demo prop

Renders ReactFlow with fitView, all interaction disabled (panOnDrag=false, zoomOnScroll=false, zoomOnPinch=false, zoomOnDoubleClick=false, nodesDraggable=false, nodesConnectable=false, elementsSelectable=false)
Container has pointerEvents: none
Uses node and edge types from the viewer (same as view-canvas.tsx)
Imports convertNode and convertConnector from lib/demo-to-flow
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add FlowCard component with mini canvas preview
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a clickable card showing a mini live preview of a flow, its name, and how long ago it was published.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/viewer/src/components/flow-card.tsx
FlowCard accepts flow: FlowListItem and onClick: () => void
Card has a 16:9 aspect-ratio container with MiniCanvas filling it on a dark (#09090b) background
Bottom bar shows flow name (truncated) on the left and relative date on the right
Relative date formats: 'today', 'yesterday', 'Nd ago', 'Nmo ago', 'Ny ago'
Entire card is a button element that calls onClick
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add /flows discovery page with grid and pagination
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse all public flows in a responsive grid with pagination.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/viewer/src/pages/flows.tsx with a FlowsPage component
Fetches GET /api/flows?page=N&limit=12 using fetchFlows from viewer-api
Grid is 1 col on mobile, 2 cols on md, 3 cols on lg
Loading state shows 12 skeleton placeholder cards matching FlowCard dimensions
Clicking a card navigates to /flow/:uuid
Pagination shows Previous / Page N of M / Next buttons; Previous disabled on page 1, Next disabled on last page; page stored in ?page= query param; scrolls to top on page change
Pagination hidden when totalPages <= 1
Empty state shows 'No flows yet.' text
Error state shows error message text
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wire /flows route and add Flows nav link to header
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reach the /flows page from the viewer header and by navigating directly to the URL.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/viewer/src/app.tsx adds <Route path='/flows' element={<FlowsPage />} /> inside the ViewerLayout wrapper alongside the existing /flow/:uuid route
apps/viewer/src/components/viewer-header.tsx shows a 'Flows' link (same style as the existing SeeFlow Studio link) pointing to /flows
The Flows link is not shown when on the /flow/:uuid route (consistent with existing isFlowView logic)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Discover recent flows section to home page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see the 6 most recent public flows on the home page so I can explore what others have shared.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/viewer/src/pages/home.tsx adds a DiscoverSection component rendered just above the footer
Section heading is 'Discover recent flows' with a 'View all →' link to /flows
Fetches GET /api/flows?page=1&limit=6 using fetchFlows on mount
Loading state: 6 skeleton cards in the same grid (1/2/3 col responsive)
Loaded state: 6 FlowCard components; clicking navigates to /flow/:uuid
If the API errors or returns 0 flows, the entire section is hidden (returns null)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add help text under Email field in export dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a brief explanation under the Email field in the Export to seeflow.dev dialog so I understand why my email is required.
acceptanceCriteria: In the Export to seeflow.dev dialog, a small helper text appears directly below the Email input field
The helper text reads: 'We'll use this to let you manage your flows in the future.'
The helper text is styled subtly (e.g., text-xs text-muted-foreground) so it doesn't compete with the input
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
